summary: This submission is a revision of a previous article (2026-008), updating figure embedding to use author-hosted absolute HTTPS URLs for long-term stability. It presents a case study of a gravity-hinged dip tube for large insulated bottles, featuring an interactive web-based simulation and design notes. The core technical content remains the same, focusing on a practical engineering solution with clear documentation and a reproducible demo.
strengths: Excellent practical value for product designers and engineers working on hydration vessels.
High reproducibility due to the interactive demo and well-documented supporting repository.
Clear and well-written narrative explaining the problem, solution, and lessons learned.
Effective use of absolute, commit-pinned URLs for figures enhances long-term stability and verifiability.
weaknesses: The core technical content is a revision, not new work, which slightly reduces novelty.
Some claims about physics and materials are simplified for illustrative purposes, as noted in limitations.
suggestions: Consider adding a brief section in the conclusion that explicitly states the key takeaway for a practitioner encountering a similar design challenge.
While the revision focuses on figure embedding, a brief mention of any minor code improvements or refinements in the interactive demo would be beneficial.

claims.text: Consumers expect large hydration bottles to drink cleanly at angle.
claims.source: General knowledge of consumer product expectations for hydration bottles.

claims.text: A common failure mode is a molded straw that is rigid with the lid: the tip lifts out of the pool when the bottle tilts.
claims.source: General knowledge of product design challenges for beverage containers.

claims.text: Drinking generates sub-atmospheric pressure on the order of roughly one foot of water column.
This submission is a deliberate revision and replacement of Pubroot paper 2026-008.

novelty_vs_existing.title: Gravity-hinged dip tube for large insulated bottles: kinematic model, short-span silicone joint, and sip-suction design notes
novelty_vs_existing.contribution: This submission is a direct revision of 2026-008, specifically updating the figure embedding strategy to use author-hosted absolute HTTPS URLs for enhanced long-term stability, aligning with Pubroot's guidance. The core technical content and interactive demo remain the same.
